title: Pre-KNN vector-path routing for a registry `filterable` attribute, via a NON-DESTRUCTIVE vec0 reshape (ADR-0.8.11 D3; TC-46 HITL Option 1)
status: COMPLETE — landed whole as one coherent RED->GREEN. TC-46 (reshape acknowledgement channel) was RESOLVED by HITL to Option 1 (NON-DESTRUCTIVE reshape) at the baseline commit ff2feb56, which dissolved the prior decomposition's blocker: a non-destructive reshape preserves embeddings, so an additive filterable ADD is NOT a ProjectionDestructive event and needs no acknowledgement parameter and no new governed surface.

commits.subject: test(0.8.20): RED — Slice 15e pre-KNN filterable vec0 routing
commits.red_evidence: 3 of 4 tests fail behaviourally at this commit (real output captured): filterable predicate does NOT compile into the phase-1 pre-KNN MATCH clause (generated SQL had no `AND attr_...=?3`); declaring a filterable projection does NOT reshape vector_default (table SQL = the shipped 6-column shape, no attr col); a filtered KNN over the absent attr column errs (Err(Storage)). The 4th (idempotent no-op) is vacuously green pre-reshape and becomes load-bearing post-GREEN.

commits.subject: feat(0.8.20): GREEN — Slice 15e non-destructive vec0 reshape + pre-KNN filterable routing

cargo_test_engine_schema.delta: +4 = the four Slice-15e tests in tests/slice15e_prekn_filterable.rs

explicit_named.unfiltered_phase1_sql_byte_identical_to_: PASS — the shipped {SourceType,Kind,CreatedAfter,Status} pre-KNN behaviour is byte-unchanged (attr_cols empty => byte-identical SQL invariant holds)

operator_feature_rebuild_erasure.command: cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features operator --test rebuild_vec0 --test rebuild_projections --test erasure_projection_registry --test erasure_completeness -- --test-threads=1
operator_feature_rebuild_erasure.note: Sanity-run because these recreate/rebuild vector_default via the async worker INSERT path I made dynamic. 10+4+4+1 = 19 passed / 0 failed.

gates.eu7: ZERO eu7 runs (R-20-EU7 CLOSED by HITL). eu7_real_corpus_ac stays #[ignore]d — it is one of the 13 ignored; a reshape/re-insert must not and did not run it.
gates.ac_041_recovery_denylist: UNCHANGED — {recover, restore, repair, fix, rebuild}; no denylist file touched.

reshape_mechanism_as_built.kind: engine-runtime reshape (NO schema-version bump; SCHEMA_VERSION stays 24). Follows the shipped migrate_vector_partition_pack1_to_pack2 precedent.
reshape_mechanism_as_built.encoding_scheme: attr_vec0_column(name) = 'attr_' + lowercase hex of the name's UTF-8 bytes. Injective (reversible by decode_attr_vec0_column), matches ^attr_[0-9a-f]+$, and cannot collide with the built-ins (embedding, embedding_bin, source_type, kind, created_at, status). vec0 rejects quoted identifiers and Slice-15d names allow spaces/unicode/'-', so a bare byte-safe identifier is mandatory. Examples pinned by the test: 'priority' -> attr_7072696f72697479, 'due date' -> attr_6475652064617465.
reshape_mechanism_as_built.reconcile: reconcile_vector_attr_columns(conn, dim) diffs the registry-DESIRED columns (desired_vector_attr_columns: one per Filterable projection, sorted) against the live-ACTUAL columns (actual_vector_attr_columns: parsed from vector_default's CREATE SQL, sorted). desired==actual => pure no-op (no reshape, no re-insert, vec0 untouched). Differ => ONE non-destructive reshape. No-op when vector_default is absent (no embedder).
reshape_mechanism_as_built.non_destructive_reshape: reshape_vector_partition_nondestructive: stage (base cols + every ACTUAL attr col) -> DROP vector_default -> recreate at DESIRED shape via vector_partition_create_sql(dim, false, desired_cols) -> re-insert. Runs on the caller's connection/tx (no nested tx), so configure_projections gets the reshape atomically with its registry mutation.
triggers: configure_projections: apply_projection_config calls reconcile after the registry mutations, inside the same write tx (drain()-gated, so no async worker is mid-write). Atomic registry+shape change => they can never diverge.

boot open: a new reconcile step after rederive_projections_on_boot, wrapped in its own tx. On a DB whose shape already matches the registry (every reopen of a DB that declared filterable projections) the diff is empty => no-op => NEVER a silent boot-time wipe.

four_conditions_proof.condition_1_rowid_explicit: The re-insert lists `rowid` verbatim in both the INSERT column list and the SELECT (INSERT INTO vector_default(rowid, embedding, embedding_bin, ...) SELECT rowid, ... FROM stage). Proven by reshape_is_nondestructive_preserves_bits_rowids_and_sentinel: `after_rowids == old_rowids` (the exact same rowids survive; no auto-assign).
four_conditions_proof.condition_2_plain_metadata_not_aux: vector_partition_create_sql appends `,{col} TEXT` (plain metadata), never `+{col}` (aux). Proven two ways: the reshape test asserts the table SQL contains `attr_7072696f72697479 TEXT` and NOT `+attr_7072696f72697479`; and filtered_knn_over_attr_column_does_not_hard_error runs a real `... MATCH ... AND attr_<hex>='low' ORDER BY distance` and gets Ok (an aux column would hard-error, exactly as pr_g10's aux_status regression documents).
four_conditions_proof.condition_3_sentinel_backfill: A DESIRED column with no ACTUAL predecessor back-fills `''` in the re-insert SELECT (vec0 TEXT metadata is NOT-NULL-able). Proven: after declaring the projection, all pre-existing rows read `''` in the new column, and the filtered KNN (`AND attr_<hex>='high'`) SKIPS every sentinel old row while MATCHING the populated new row.
four_conditions_proof.condition_4_verbatim_bits_THE_TRAP: embedding_bin is copied VERBATIM via `vec_bit(embedding_bin)` in the re-insert — NEVER re-derived from the raw `embedding`. Proven directly: reshape_is_nondestructive_preserves_bits_rowids_and_sentinel snapshots each old row's embedding_bin bytes BEFORE the reshape and asserts they are BYTE-IDENTICAL after (read_vector_bin_for_test), AND that each old row's vec_distance_hamming to a fixed query is UNCHANGED across the reshape, AND that old + new Hamming distances all lie in [0, dim] (same comparable scale). This is the silently-wrong trap the mandate flagged; the test would go red if the bits were re-quantized under a different centering. run_pin_and_requantize_pass (the SEPARATE same-shape re-quantize op) was left with its re-quantize logic untouched — only its INSERT was extended to PRESERVE existing attr-col values.

prekn_compile_proof.generated_sql_attribute_only: WITH candidates AS ( SELECT rowid FROM vector_default WHERE embedding_bin MATCH vec_quantize_binary(vec_f32(?1)) AND attr_7072696f72697479=?3 ORDER BY distance LIMIT 192 ) SELECT c.rowid, vec_distance_l2(v.embedding, vec_f32(?2)) AS l2 FROM candidates c JOIN vector_default v ON v.rowid = c.rowid ORDER BY l2 LIMIT 10
prekn_compile_proof.assertions: The `AND attr_7072696f72697479=?3` predicate sits INSIDE the phase-1 candidates MATCH block (before ORDER BY distance) => pre-KNN, D3-conformant. The generated SQL contains NO `json_extract` (never demoted post-KNN). Combined with a metadata field, canonical order is preserved: `AND kind=?3` then `AND attr_...=?4`. A space-containing name ('due date') encodes to attr_6475652064617465 with no quoted identifier.

idempotency_proof.what: Declare filterable('priority') twice. First apply: delta.built contains 'priority' (reshape happens). Second identical apply: delta.unchanged == true, AND the table SQL is byte-unchanged, AND the rowids are byte-unchanged, AND every row's embedding_bin is byte-unchanged (no re-insert). Structural (diff vs the live vec0 columns), so boot re-derive replaying the same set is the same no-op.

made_dynamic: async embed worker commit_projection_outcomes (INSERT OR IGNORE) — binds attr cols from the node body, read from canonical_nodes by write_cursor ONLY when attr cols exist (the common no-filterable hot path stays byte-identical and does NO extra lookup).
write_vector_for_test (#[doc(hidden)] test helper) — no JSON body, so binds the '' sentinel for every attr col.

run_pin_and_requantize_pass (DELETE+INSERT re-quantize) — reads the live attr cols + this row's values BEFORE the DELETE and re-binds them; the re-quantize (centering) logic is untouched. This is the SEPARATE same-shape op the mandate said to leave alone re: bits — its INSERT still had to bind every column or vec0 errors.
left_base_shape_no_binding: migrate_vector_partition_pack1_to_pack2 — a 0.8.9-era pack1 DB predates the projection registry (schema step 24), so it has no filterable set => base shape; create_sql passes &[]. Boot reconcile adds any attr cols afterwards if the registry warrants.
enumerated_insert_sites.invariant: Every dynamic INSERT and vector_partition_create_sql produces BYTE-IDENTICAL SQL to the shipped statement when the attr-column set is empty (verified by the whole 622-test base staying green plus the byte-identity pin).

search_reachability.carrier: SearchFilter gained a pub `attributes: Vec<(String,String)>` field (name,value). vector_filter_clause/vector_filter_values lower each to `AND attr_<hex>=?n` AFTER the four metadata fields; is_unfiltered accounts for it (empty => unfiltered byte-identical path preserved). search_filtered carries attributes across the existing Filter round-trip (the unified Filter/FilterTerm grammar does NOT yet carry an attribute term — a later slice can add that user-facing surface; NOT in 15e scope).
search_reachability.note: No FilterTerm variant was added, so slice40's `filterterm_enum_is_exactly_five_variants` stays green.

governed_surface_delta.engine_public_api: SearchFilter gained a pub field `attributes` (additive). New pub-but-#[doc(hidden)] test seams (read_vector_bin_for_test, query_i64_col_for_test, query_text_col_for_test). attr_vec0_column/decode are private.
governed_surface_delta.bindings: fathomdb-py + fathomdb-napi RustSearchFilter constructors updated to `..Default::default()` (4 sites). This is BEHAVIOUR-NEUTRAL glue: NO new user-facing filter field reaches the bindings (SearchFilterInput / the Python SearchFilter are UNCHANGED, still 4 fields), and `attributes` is never populated from JS/Py. Per the task's own qualifier ('a new filter term reaching the bindings' is what triggers X1), this is NOT a binding-surface change, so X1/pyright were NOT re-run. The glue is compile-verified by `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` (exit 0). FLAGGED for reviewer judgment: if the reviewer deems any binding-crate .rs edit to require X1, a fresh-clone X1 + pyright run is the follow-up (never in this worktree per the maturin/pip prohibition).
governed_surface_delta.requirement_ids_and_tests: R-20-PR / ADR-0.8.11 D3. Pinned by tests/slice15e_prekn_filterable.rs (4 tests). No new AC minted (per TC-42/acceptance-md-locked: track by requirement-id + TDD test names).

what_the_plan_or_prior_decomposition_got: The prior decomposition (commit cf2f155c) assumed the reshape MUST wipe embeddings (its 'decision 4: NO blob preservation'), which forced an acknowledgement-channel design question (TC-15E-ACK) and a ~6-site + bindings vertical. TC-46's HITL resolution to a NON-DESTRUCTIVE reshape (embeddings preserved) dissolved that entirely: an additive filterable ADD preserves data => not ProjectionDestructive => no ack parameter, no governed configure_projections signature change. The slice landed whole.

The prior decomposition listed `rebuild_vec0 insert path — lib.rs:7441` and `embedder-profile mean-pin write path — lib.rs:7096` as distinct INSERT sites. In fact rebuild_vec0 re-enqueues embeds that flow through the SAME async-worker INSERT (commit_projection_outcomes), so making that one site dynamic covers rebuild; and lib.rs:7096 is write_vector_for_test (a test helper), not a mean-pin production path. The true distinct raw INSERT-INTO-vector_default sites are 5 (2 of which are pre-registry migrations that stay base-shape), so 3 needed dynamic binding — fewer than the '~6' estimate.

The reshape does NOT back-fill the new vec0 attr column from existing node bodies — it uses the '' sentinel (condition #3), so the pre-KNN filter only sees rows written AFTER the projection was declared. This is an intentional asymmetry with the Slice-15d EAV backfill (which DOES backfill canonical_attributes from bodies). It matches the mandate's condition #3 + the ALIGNMENT proof exactly, but consumers should know: for full pre-KNN coverage of pre-existing rows, a rebuild/re-embed (which re-extracts from the body on the async path) is required. Noted as a deliberate, documented deferred-population state (mirrors status@Slice-10).

fix-1.title: Close TWO P2 codex §9 findings in the new attribute-filter path: (1) attribute filter not applied on the TEXT/FTS arm ⇒ hybrid false positives; (2) existing rows get the '' sentinel even when their body HAS the attribute ⇒ false negatives.

commits.subject: test(0.8.20): RED — Slice 15e fix-1 total attribute filter + existing-row backfill
commits.red_evidence:
Both FAILED at baseline (real captured output):
  F1 hybrid_fts_arm_applies_attribute_filter — panicked 'priority=low doc must NOT leak through the FTS arm: ["{...\"priority\":\"high\"}", "{...\"priority\":\"low\"}"]'. The priority=low MISS doc matched the FTS query and, because text_hit_passes_filter checked only the four legacy metadata fields, leaked through the FTS arm of the hybrid search (the vector arm correctly pruned it — projection declared BEFORE the writes so vec0 attr_<hex> was populated at write time; the sanity assertion 'MISS vec0 column populated from body' == ["low"] passed, isolating the leak to the FTS arm).
  F2 existing_row_backfilled... — panicked 'assertion `left == right` failed: pre-existing row whose body HAS the attribute must back-fill its real value, not \'\'  left: [""]  right: ["high"]'. The PRESENT row (body priority=high, written+embedded BEFORE the projection) got the blanket '' sentinel in vec0 from reshape_vector_partition_nondestructive, so it was a pre-KNN false negative despite canonical_attributes having already been back-filled to 'high'.

commits.subject: feat(0.8.20): GREEN — Slice 15e fix-1 attribute filter total across arms + backfill existing rows

finding_1_total_filter_across_arms.problem: SearchFilter.attributes was applied ONLY to the vector phase-1 pre-KNN SQL (attr_<hex>=? via vector_filter_clause). The FTS/text branches (text_hit_passes_filter / edge_fts_hit_passes_filter) checked only source_type/kind/created_after/status, so in a hybrid RRF search a doc returned by the FTS arm that FAILS the attribute filter still surfaced — violating ADR-0.8.11 D3 total backend-dispatch.
finding_1_total_filter_across_arms.fix: Added a shared private helper hit_attributes_pass_filter(tx, id, filter) that, for each (attr_name, value) in filter.attributes, reads attr_value from the canonical_attributes EAV table (SELECT attr_value FROM canonical_attributes WHERE write_cursor=?1 AND attr_name=?2 LIMIT 1). An absent attribute has no row ⇒ reads as the '' sentinel via unwrap_or_default(), so a non-empty equality fails-to-match. Wired into BOTH text_hit_passes_filter (keyed by node id) and edge_fts_hit_passes_filter (keyed by write_cursor) just before their `Ok(true)`.
finding_1_total_filter_across_arms.match_with_vector_arm: canonical_attributes is populated by project_one_attribute via the SAME extract_scalar_attribute that fills the vec0 attr_<hex> column, and (after finding 2) the reshape also back-fills the vec0 column from canonical_attributes — so the EAV value and the vec0 value are IDENTICAL by construction. Absent ⇒ '' on both arms. Hybrid RRF is therefore coherent: the exact same rows pass/fail on the FTS arm and the vector arm.
finding_1_total_filter_across_arms.edges_verified: Edge bodies ARE projected into vector_default (rowid == write_cursor) with the same attr_<hex> columns, and Slice 15d projects an edge's filterable attributes into canonical_attributes keyed by its write_cursor, so edge_fts_hit_passes_filter was extended too (same helper, keyed by write_cursor); an edge carrying no such attribute reads '' and fails a non-empty equality exactly as on the vector arm.

finding_2_existing_row_backfill.problem: reshape_vector_partition_nondestructive filled EVERY newly-added attr column for existing rows with the blanket '' sentinel. But apply_projection_config runs backfill_attribute (which fills canonical_attributes from each active row's body) at lib.rs:15934/15946 BEFORE reconcile_vector_attr_columns triggers the reshape at :15966. So a pre-existing row whose body contained the attribute was written '' in vec0 and failed every pre-KNN attribute filter until re-embedded — an immediate false negative.
finding_2_existing_row_backfill.fix: In the re-insert SELECT, a NEW desired column (no ACTUAL predecessor) now back-fills from the already-populated canonical_attributes instead of a blanket '': decode_attr_vec0_column(col) → attr_name, then COALESCE((SELECT ca.attr_value FROM canonical_attributes ca WHERE ca.write_cursor = _fathomdb_vector_reshape_stage.rowid AND ca.attr_name = '<escaped name>' LIMIT 1), ''). execute_batch takes no bind params, so the decoded name is embedded as a SQL string literal with single-quotes doubled; a column that fails to decode falls back to '' rather than panicking. The '' sentinel (condition #3) is now used ONLY where the attribute is genuinely absent (no canonical_attributes row).
finding_2_existing_row_backfill.condition_3_still_holds: The genuinely-absent case still yields '' (COALESCE default), so vec0 TEXT metadata never NULLs/errors and an absent row cleanly fails-to-match. F2 proves both halves: PRESENT (body priority=high) vec0 attr col reads 'high'; ABSENT (no priority) reads '' and is excluded from a priority==high filter.

four_tc46_conditions_still_hold.condition_1_rowid_explicit: UNCHANGED — the re-insert still lists `rowid` verbatim in both the INSERT column list and the SELECT. reshape_is_nondestructive_preserves_bits_rowids_and_sentinel stays green (after_rowids == old_rowids).
four_tc46_conditions_still_hold.condition_2_plain_metadata_not_aux: UNCHANGED — vector_partition_create_sql still appends `{col} TEXT`, never `+{col}`. filtered_knn_over_attr_column_does_not_hard_error stays green.
four_tc46_conditions_still_hold.condition_3_sentinel_only_when_absent: REFINED — sentinel is now the ABSENT case only (back-fill from canonical_attributes otherwise), exactly as the mandate specifies. reshape_is_nondestructive_...'s OLD1/OLD2 rows (bodies WITHOUT the attribute) still correctly read '' and are skipped by the filter, so that test stays green.
four_tc46_conditions_still_hold.condition_4_verbatim_bits_THE_TRAP: UNCHANGED — embedding_bin is still copied VERBATIM via `vec_bit(embedding_bin)` in the re-insert; NO re-quantization. My only change to the re-insert was the VALUE SOURCE of the new attr columns (canonical_attributes instead of blanket ''). reshape_is_nondestructive_preserves_bits_rowids_and_sentinel (byte-identical embedding_bin + unchanged Hamming distances across the reshape) stays green, confirming the silent-recall-corruption trap is not reopened.

cargo_test_engine_schema.delta: +2 = the two fix-1 tests in tests/slice15e_prekn_filterable.rs (slice15e now 6 tests)

pr_g10_filtered_knn.unfiltered_phase1_sql_byte_identical_to_: PASS — the byte-identity of the unfiltered pre-KNN SQL is unchanged (fix-1 touches only the text/FTS filter check and the reshape re-insert VALUE source, not vector_filter_clause / build_vector_phase1_sql).
pr_g10_filtered_knn.other_named: aux_status_column_hard_errors_under_knn_where PASS; filtered_phase1_sql_appends_present_predicates_only PASS; sentinel_backfills_status_on_simulated_pack1_db PASS; filter_prunes_vector_candidates_by_kind PASS; status_filter_prunes_all_because_population_is_null_only PASS.

operator_feature_rebuild_erasure.command: cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features operator --test rebuild_vec0 --test rebuild_projections --test erasure_projection_registry --test erasure_completeness -- --test-threads=1
operator_feature_rebuild_erasure.note: Sanity-run because finding 2 changes the reshape re-insert path that rebuild exercises.

gates.bindings_X1_pyright: NOT re-run — CORRECTLY skipped. fix-1 changes are 100% engine-internal (only src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs changed; `git diff --stat` = 1 file, +90/-7). No binding crate touched, no new SearchFilter field, SearchFilter.attributes remains NOT reachable from the binding input types (SearchFilterInput / Python SearchFilter unchanged). Per the mandate's own qualifier, this is not a binding-surface change ⇒ no fresh-clone X1/pyright, no .pyi gap reintroduced.
gates.eu7: ZERO eu7 runs. eu7_real_corpus_ac stays #[ignore]d (one of the unchanged 13 ignored).
gates.ac_041_recovery_denylist: UNCHANGED — {recover, restore, repair, fix, rebuild} = exactly five; no denylist file touched. AC-041 green.
gates.governed_surface: PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED — no new public API, no new AC minted, never HITL-SIGNED. The only new symbol is a private fn (hit_attributes_pass_filter).

files_touched: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs — hit_attributes_pass_filter (new private fn); text_hit_passes_filter + edge_fts_hit_passes_filter (attr check wired in); reshape_vector_partition_nondestructive (new-column back-fill from canonical_attributes)

follow_ups_non_blocking: Perf: when attr columns exist, the async worker does `SELECT body FROM canonical_nodes WHERE write_cursor=?` per insert; there is no index on canonical_nodes(write_cursor) (per the shipped fix-3 note), so this is a scan bounded by corpus size. Opt-in (only with a filterable projection declared). A write_cursor index is a candidate future optimization.

Surface: a user-facing unified FilterTerm::Attribute variant (so callers can request an attribute filter through the Filter grammar / bindings) is deferred to a later slice; 15e delivers the routing mechanism reachable via SearchFilter.attributes + the generated-SQL seam.
Coverage: backfilling the vec0 attr column for pre-existing rows (vs the '' sentinel) on a rebuild/re-embed, if full pre-KNN coverage of historical rows is wanted.
